title: Mohammad Sayeef Alam
text:
I am currently pursuing innovative research on the genetics of celiac disease as a PhD candidate in the Department of Public Health and Nursing at the Norwegian University of Science and Technology. My academic foundation is built upon dual master’s degrees - a Master of Philosophy and a Master of Science in Biostatistics and Demography - earned from the International Institute for Population Sciences.
I’ve translated theoretical knowledge of statistical methods into practical applications in the field of Health Economics Outcome Research. As an Associate in the Advanced Analytics team at Parexel, I honed my skills in data-driven decision making and statistical analysis within the pharmaceutical industry.
My expertise lies at the intersection of applied statistics, data analytics, and healthcare. I excel in developing and implementing sophisticated statistical models for genomic data analysis, with a particular focus on advancing the field of precision medicine. Additionally, I’ve demonstrated proficiency in conducting economic evaluations of cutting-edge drugs and therapies, providing valuable insights for healthcare policy and resource allocation.
I am passionate about leveraging programming and statistical software to solve complex healthcare challenges. My proficiency in R and RShiny allows me to create interactive data visualizations and develop robust statistical pipelines, enhancing both the efficiency and interpretability of research outcomes.




Advanced statistical analysis and modeling
Genomic data interpretation and manipulation
Health economic modeling and outcome research
R/RShiny development for interactive data applications
Machine learning applications in healthcare
Data-driven decision making in clinical settings


Through my multidisciplinary background and hands-on experience, I bring a unique blend of statistical rigor, programming proficiency, and health economics expertise to tackle complex challenges in the evolving landscape of personalized medicine and healthcare analytics.

title: Projects
text:
The Nordic Shortfall Calculator is a web application that allows user to explore and analyze the quality adjusted life expectancy for the Nordic populations (Denmark, Finland, Norway and Sweden).
Tuning the parameter produces adjusted estimates for different outcomes like Absolute shortfall, Proportional shortfal HRQoL and Cummulative QALY and survival.
Shiny for R application helps develop app which can deployed at server and then understood by stakeholders easily by interacting with it.
If you would like to get a demo, kindly reach out.
